common_assertions: EPIC.md contains a Story Map table with ID, Title, Priority, Size, Blocked by columns
Every story in STORIES.md has a Priority field with a MoSCoW value (Must, Should, Could, Won't)
Every story in STORIES.md has a Size field with a numeric day estimate of 1, 2, or 3
Every story in STORIES.md has a Blocked by field (may be em-dash or list)
Every story in STORIES.md has a User value statement in the form 'As a <role>, I can <action> so that <outcome>'
STORIES.md contains at least one Gherkin scenario (Given/When/Then) per story
Story IDs are zero-padded three-digit identifiers like S-001
Every Must-priority FR from the source BRD is covered by at least one story
Stories are sequenced with a walking-skeleton story first (touches multiple layers end to end)
Output uses canonical terms from the source BRD without inventing new names

evals.name: thin-brd-teampulse
evals.prompt: Convert the BRD at BRD.md into an Epic and Stories using vertical-slice architecture. Output EPIC.md and STORIES.md at the repo root.
evals.expected_output: EPIC.md + STORIES.md derived from a thin BRD with 3 FRs and several open questions. Skill should produce a walking-skeleton story first, slice each Must into ~3-day stories, surface unresolved questions, and not invent priorities or AC the BRD did not specify.
specific_assertions: EPIC.md Open Questions section preserves the unresolved BRD questions (reminder channel, team definition) rather than inventing answers
Pulse-submission and manager-dashboard FRs are each represented by at least one story
If reminders story exists, it is marked Should priority (matching FR-3)
No more than ~6 stories total (BRD is small and intentionally thin)

evals.name: multi-capability-mercatorpay
evals.prompt: Convert the BRD at BRD.md into an Epic and Stories using vertical-slice architecture. Output EPIC.md and STORIES.md at the repo root.
evals.expected_output: EPIC.md + STORIES.md for a marketplace BRD with 9 FRs spanning seller onboarding, checkout, payouts, tax, admin. Skill should slice each Must FR into multiple 3-day vertical stories (e.g. checkout-with-tax becomes several stories), sequence with walking skeleton first, identify blockers between Stripe Connect setup, KYC, payouts, etc.
specific_assertions: Checkout FR (FR-4) is decomposed into multiple slices (e.g. single-item checkout, EU VAT, US sales tax, receipt) rather than one monolithic story
Seller KYC story (FR-1) exists and blocks the seller payout story (FR-6)
Map upload story (FR-2) includes a chunked-upload acceptance criterion or is split to defer chunked uploads
Walking-skeleton story exists that exercises auth + one round trip through buyer or seller flow
Stripe Connect KYC is listed as a blocker or dependency for any payout-related story
Total story count between ~12 and ~25 (sufficient slicing for 9 FRs without ticket explosion)

evals.name: real-cyprian-translation
evals.prompt: Convert the BRD at BRD.md into an Epic and Stories using vertical-slice architecture. Output EPIC.md and STORIES.md at the repo root.
evals.expected_output: EPIC.md + STORIES.md for the GrapheneOS translation app (12 BRs, mobile, OCR, STT, offline fallback). Skill should slice across modalities (text/audio/image), order risky integrations (LibreTranslate, EasyNMT, PaddleOCR) appropriately, produce Gherkin AC, and produce a walking-skeleton story that proves end-to-end on Pixel 9 Pro.
specific_assertions: Walking-skeleton story exercises end-to-end translation on GrapheneOS / Pixel 9 Pro (e.g. text input -> LibreTranslate -> displayed translation)
Each modality (text BR-003, audio BR-004, image/OCR BR-005) has its own dedicated story or slice set
Server preference (BR-008) and offline fallback (BR-009) are separate stories with the offline story blocked by or sequenced after the server story
TLS/encryption requirement (BR-010) is captured either as a cross-cutting NFR story or baked into AC of network-touching stories
Google-free constraint (BR-001) appears in either a dedicated guardrail story or in AC of the walking-skeleton story
